data.description:

The latest update is out from Talgo S.A. ( (ES:TLGO) ).

Talgo S.A. has announced the resignation of Mr. Jose María Oriol Fabra, who served as the Non-Executive Vice President of the Board of Directors, effective immediately, due to personal reasons. This change in leadership might influence the strategic direction of the company, potentially impacting its governance and decision-making processes.

More about Talgo S.A.

Talgo S.A. is a company operating in the transportation industry, primarily focused on designing and manufacturing high-speed trains and railway technology. The company is known for its innovative solutions and commitment to efficiency within the rail transport sector.

data.description:
Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

Celtic ( (GB:CCP) ) just unveiled an update.

Celtic PLC has announced changes to its issued share capital following the conversion of 5,300 Convertible Cumulative Preference Shares. The company’s current share capital comprises various types of shares, with the Ordinary Shares and Convertible Preferred Ordinary Shares being significant for shareholders in terms of calculating their voting rights. Notably, the new Ordinary Shares resulting from these conversions are set to commence trading on AIM on 19 February 2025, aligning with the existing shares in all respects.

More about Celtic

COPRO-HOLDINGS Co., Ltd. ( (JP:7059) ) just unveiled an announcement.

COPRO-HOLDINGS Co., Ltd. reported steady progress in its core construction technician dispatching business, with consolidated sales mostly aligning with plans despite a slowdown in the IT engineer dispatch segment. The company is preparing for its next fiscal year, aiming to exceed its Medium-Term Management Plan targets by revising upward its performance goals, demonstrating strong confidence in its future growth trajectory.

More about COPRO-HOLDINGS Co., Ltd.

COPRO-HOLDINGS Co., Ltd. operates in the construction industry, primarily focusing on the dispatching of construction technicians. The company also engages in the dispatching of IT engineers, although this segment showed sluggish performance recently.

Linical Co., Ltd. ( (JP:2183) ) has issued an update.

Linical Co., Ltd. reported a significant decline in its financial performance for the nine months ending December 31, 2024, with a 14% drop in net sales compared to the previous year. The company also faced operating and ordinary income losses, alongside a negative profit attributable to its owners, indicating a challenging economic environment and potentially impacting its market positioning and stakeholder confidence.

Linical Co., Ltd. is a company listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, focusing on providing comprehensive clinical research services. It operates in the healthcare and pharmaceutical industry, offering solutions that support the clinical development and regulatory approval processes.

data.title: Nippon Steel’s Subsidiary Osaka Steel to Buy Back Shares
data.description:
Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The latest announcement is out from NIPPON STEEL ( (JP:5401) ).

Nippon Steel Corporation has announced that its consolidated subsidiary, Osaka Steel Co., Ltd., will proceed with a tender offer to repurchase its own shares, setting the purchase price at 2,450 yen per share. Nippon Steel has decided to tender all its holdings in Osaka Steel’s common stock, aligning with strategic decisions made during the board meetings held on January 31 and February 14, 2025.

More about NIPPON STEEL

Nippon Steel Corporation operates in the steel industry and is a leading producer of steel products. The company focuses on manufacturing a wide range of steel products that cater to various industries, including construction, automotive, and infrastructure projects.
